preamble: The shift from the velar nasal [ŋ] to the uvular nasal [ɴ] is a form of retraction, moving the nasal articulation further back in the vocal tract.

examples.note: Moraic nasal retraction in final position.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
